About Me

Data is my canvas.

I'm Herbert Cheruiyot, a Data Analyst at AGC Tenwek Hospital (Strategy & Projects Department), Bomet, Kenya. I hold a BSc. in Actuarial Science from Kabarak University (2023) and specialise in transforming raw operational data into actionable insights.

Skilled in Excel, Power BI, SQL, and Python, I support strategic decision-making through KPI tracking, Balanced Scorecard development, interactive dashboards, and performance reporting.

Projects

Work that ships

01 · ML · Forecasting · Clustering
BMW Sales Analysis
Full end-to-end data science project on a global BMW sales dataset — 50,000 records, $3.75B total revenue, 253M+ units sold across all major world regions. Built entirely in Python on Google Colab.
  • Regional Pricing: All 6 regions priced $73K–$76K — Asia leads, Middle East trails. BMW maintains a consistent global premium with <$3K spread across continents.
  • Top Models: 7 Series leads sales (~24M units); all top 10 models sit within a tight 22M–24M band — no single model dominates, showing balanced portfolio demand.
  • Sales Trend (2014–2024): Peak at 3.05M units in 2021 (COVID recovery surge), followed by a sharp 2023 correction, then strong 2024 rebound.
  • YoY Decoupling: Sales volume swings ±9% year-to-year while average price growth holds near-flat (±2%) — BMW actively shields its pricing power regardless of volume.
  • Revenue Combos: M3·Asia·Hybrid tops at $16.95M. Hybrid variants dominate 8 of the top 10 combos — hybrid demand is the clearest revenue signal in the dataset.
  • Profitability Heatmap: i3 in Africa scores 34 (price-per-KM) — far above all other segments (avg 2–6). A notable outlier with the highest margin proxy in the entire portfolio.
  • Correlation Analysis: All inter-feature correlations are near zero — no linear predictor dominates, validating the choice of Random Forest over regression models.
  • Feature Importance (RF): Sales Volume (0.29) → Mileage_KM (0.25) → Mileage/Year (0.25) → Engine Size (0.16) → Car Age (0.05). Volume drives price more than engine size.
  • SHAP Global (500 cars): Mileage_KM is the single strongest driver (~1,650 mean |SHAP|). Car_Age the weakest. Consistent with RF importance but reveals non-linear direction of effects.
  • SHAP Force Plot (Car #16134): Predicted $78,251 vs $75K baseline. Car_Age and Mileage/Year push price up; Sales_Volume pulls it slightly down. Engine size = largest absolute contributor.
  • Price vs Fuel Type: All fuel types scatter uniformly across $40K–$120K — fuel type is not a standalone price differentiator for BMW.
  • Forecasting: 4 Prophet models (base, additive, regressor, scaled) project 2025–2027 sales with confidence bands and regressor decomposition.
PythonPandasPlotly scikit-learnProphetSHAP Random ForestKMeansSeaborn

02 · Healthcare Analytics · BI Reporting
Hospital Admissions Analytics
Dual-dashboard analytics project covering 150 patient admissions across 7 departments (Jan–Apr 2025) at AGC Tenwek Hospital. Built in both Power BI and Excel to track clinical and financial KPIs for strategic decision-making.
  • KPI Cards: 150 admissions · Ksh 6M total treatment cost · 8-day avg stay · Ksh 38,762 avg per patient · 0.03% mortality rate
  • Admissions by Department: General Medicine leads at 17% · Maternity, Oncology & Pediatrics each at 16% · Cardiology lowest at 9%
  • Treatment Cost by Department: General Medicine highest at Ksh 990,662 · Cardiology lowest at Ksh 572,811
  • Monthly Trend: Peak in January (53 admissions) · Steady decline through April · Consistent care delivery despite volume changes
  • Patient Outcomes: 83% Recovered · 14% Referred · 3% Deceased — mortality primarily linked to Oncology
  • Doctor Performance: Avg treatment cost per doctor ranges Ksh 31,940–Ksh 46,312 · Dr Njoroge highest biller, Dr Mutua lowest
  • Interactive Filters: Slice by Month, Department, Doctor Assigned & Outcome across both dashboards
  • Key Insight: Avg stay stable at 8 days despite rising admissions — signals consistent clinical capacity management
Power BIExcel DAXData Modelling KPI ReportingHealthcare Analytics

BMW Sales Analysis · 13 Charts
Sales Dashboard Visuals
GitHub ↗
Average BMW Price by Region
All six regions sit tightly within the $73K–$76K band. Asia edges ahead while Middle East trails slightly — confirming BMW's consistent global premium pricing strategy with minimal regional deviation.
Average Price by Model (Filtered)
Prices cluster between $73K–$77K across all 11 models. The 3 Series leads marginally; 7 Series and X5 follow closely. Tight spread confirms BMW's deliberate single-tier premium positioning regardless of model.
Top 10 Best-Selling BMW Models
7 Series leads total sales volume (~24M units), closely followed by i8, X1 and 3 Series. All top 10 models sit within a narrow 22M–24M band — no single model dominates, reflecting balanced portfolio demand.
Sales Volume Trend Over Years (2014–2024)
Sales peaked in 2021 at ~3.05M units then dipped sharply through 2023 before rebounding strongly into 2024. The 2016–2018 trough and 2021 peak highlight the COVID recovery surge and subsequent normalisation.
YoY Growth: Sales Volume vs Average Price
Sales volume (blue) swings wildly — from -9% to +8% — while average price growth (orange) stays near-flat across the entire 2010–2024 period. BMW clearly protects price stability regardless of volume swings.
Price vs Mileage by Fuel Type
All four fuel types (Electric, Petrol, Diesel, Hybrid) scatter uniformly across $40K–$120K with no clear mileage-driven price gradient. Fuel type alone does not meaningfully segment BMW pricing — mileage effect is weak.
Top 10 Highest Revenue Combos (Model + Region + Fuel Type)
M3·Asia·Hybrid tops the table at $16.95M total revenue. Hybrid variants dominate 8 of the top 10 combos — signalling strong hybrid demand. Asia and Europe are the highest-revenue markets for BMW premium segments.
BMW Profitability Heatmap — Avg Price per KM by Model & Region
i3 in Africa stands out dramatically (value 34) — the highest price-per-KM retention of any segment. Darker cells = better profitability proxy. Most model-region combos cluster in the 2–6 range, revealing i3 as an outlier.
What Drives BMW Price? (Correlation Heatmap)
All inter-feature correlations are near zero (–0.005 to +0.009). No single variable strongly predicts BMW price in isolation — confirming that tree-based models (Random Forest) are better suited than linear approaches.
What Drives BMW Price the Most? (Feature Importance)
Sales Volume is the top predictor (0.29 importance score), followed by Mileage_KM (0.25) and Mileage_per_Year (0.25). Engine_Size_L contributes 0.16 while Car_Age has the least influence (0.05).
Global Feature Importance — SHAP Across 500 Cars
SHAP global analysis on 500 cars confirms Mileage_KM as the single strongest driver (~1,650 mean |SHAP|), with Sales_Volume and Mileage_per_Year close behind. Car_Age exerts the weakest influence at ~650.
SHAP Summary — How Features Impact Price (Red = High, Blue = Low)
Beeswarm plot across 500 cars: Red dots (high feature value) cluster on the positive SHAP side for Mileage_KM and Sales_Volume, pushing prices up. Blue dots (low values) drag prices down. Car_Age shows the tightest, most neutral spread.
SHAP Force Plot + Feature Contributions — Car #16134
For Car #16134: predicted price $78,251.82 vs base $75K. Car_Age (+) and Mileage_per_Year (+) push the price up; Sales_Volume (–) pulls it down slightly. Engine_Size_L contributes the largest absolute impact in the waterfall.
1 / 13
BMW Sales Analysis · Forecasting
BMW Sales Forecast 2025–2027
GitHub ↗
Prophet Forecast 2010–2027 (Base Model)
Base Prophet model with multiplicative seasonality. Black dots = historical data, blue line = forecast, shaded band = 95% confidence interval. Projects a continued upward trend through 2027.
Improved Forecast — Additive Model, No Negatives
Additive seasonality with floor clipping at zero. Removes unrealistic negative dips from the base model and produces a cleaner, more stable signal for planning.
Forecast Components — Trend & Seasonality
Decomposes the forecast into trend and yearly seasonality. Shows the long-run growth trajectory and which months seasonally peak in BMW sales volume.
Forecast with External Regressors
Prophet enhanced with avg_price and North America market share as external regressors — capturing macroeconomic signals to improve 2025–2027 projection accuracy.
Regressor Component Analysis
How avg_price and NA market share individually shift the forecast. Isolates the economic and geographic effects on BMW sales volume.
Final Model — Scaled Regressors + 3% Price Growth
Most sophisticated model: standardised regressors with a 3% annual price growth assumption. Prevents regressor dominance and produces the most calibrated forecast.
Final Model — Full Component Decomposition
Complete breakdown: trend, yearly seasonality, and individual contributions of scaled avg_price and NA market share shown separately.
1 / 7
Hospital Admissions Analytics · Power BI & Excel
Hospital Admissions Dashboards
GitHub ↗
Power BI Dashboard — Hospital Admissions Overview (Jan–Apr 2025)
KPI cards: 150 admissions · 6M total treatment cost · 8-day avg stay · Ksh 39K avg per patient · 0.03% mortality rate. Bar charts show General Medicine leading admissions (17%) and highest cost (Ksh 990K). Donut: 83% Recovered · 14% Referred · 3% Deceased. Filterable by Month, Department & Doctor.
Excel Dashboard — Departmental & Doctor Performance Analysis
Detailed Excel view with slicers for Department, Month, Doctor & Outcome. Horizontal bar shows avg treatment cost by doctor (Ksh 31,940–46,312) — Dr Njoroge highest biller, Dr Mutua lowest. Patient outcomes pie: 82.67% Recovered · 14% Referred · 3.33% Deceased. Monthly trend confirms Jan peak (53) with April tail-off to 1.
